LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT REGULO SIERRA'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DENIED. TO THE EXTENT THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS FILED AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION PER CCP 425.16, CROSS-DEFENDANT PRESENTS NO LAW OR EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS DIRECTED AT PROTECTED ACTIVITY. (CCP 425.16(B). SEE POINTS & AUTH. P. 5:19-24.) WHEN ONE HAS AGREED NOT TO PURSUE FUTURE LITIGATION, DOING SO IS NOT PROTECTED ACTIVITY FOR PURPOSES OF THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. ANSWER MUST BE FILED BY JUNE 10, 2013. ONE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT REGULO SIERRA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 8, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT REGULO SIERRA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT APPEAR TIME-BARRED ON ITS FACE. A DEMURRER CHALLENGES DEFECTS THAT APPEAR ON THE FACE OF THE PLEADING OR FROM MATTERS OUTSIDE THE PLEADING THAT ARE JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE. (BLANK V. KIRWAN (1985) 39 CAL.3D 311, 318.) CROSS-DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS THAT THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS A FORM OF FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE (POINTS & AUTH., P. 5:14-18), MOOT (ID. AT P. 6:2-5), TIME-BARRED (ID. AT P. 6:7-14), UNCONSCIONABLE (ID. AT P. 6:16 -7:2), AND (4) UNRIPE (ID. AT P. 7:4-6) DO NOT APPEAR ON THE FACE OF THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, BUT RATHER ARE BASED ON EXTRINSIC FACTS THAT ARE NOT JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE. EVIDENCE THAT IS NOT CONTAINED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS IMPROPER ON DEMURRER AND NOT CONSIDERED BY THE COURT. ANSWER MUST BE FILED BY JUNE 10, 2013. ONE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SIERRA, REGULO JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT INFORMED MISS HEWLETT AND MR. MURAUSKI THAT IT IS ILLEGAL FOR SOMEONE WHO IS NOT AN ATTORNEY TO REPRESENT A PARTY IN A LAWSUIT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF REGULO SIERRA'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF RULING OF DEMURRER IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PRESENT ANY NEW LAW OR FACTS UPON WHICH TO BASE THE RECONSIDERATION MOTION. THE MOTION FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CCP 1008. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ADD JUDGE KAHN AS A DOE DEFENDANT IS ALSO DENIED. IT APPEARS THAT MR SIERRA'S COMPLAINT WITH JUDGE KAHN IS THAT JUDGE KAHN DECIDED EARLIER MOTIONS INCORRECTLY; JUDICIAL IMMUNITY BARS SUCH CLAIMS IN ANY EVENT. MR. SIERRA ALSO APPEARS TO MAKE A MOTION TO DISQUALIFY JUDGE KAHN UNDER CCP 170.6. WHILE THAT IS MOOT FOR THIS MOTION, DECIDED BY A DIFFERENT JUDGE, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY, BROUGHT LONG AFTER MR. SIERRA LEARNED JUDGE KAHN WOULD HEAR MATTERS IN THIS CASE. MR. SIERRA IS REMINDED THAT HIS PAPERS ARE EXCESSIVE IN LENGTH. SEE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1103 (D)(15 PAGES FOR OPENING MEMORANDUM AND 20 PAGES FOR REPLY PAPERS). I HAVE IN ANY EVENT REVIEWED THESE PAPERS, BUT IN THE FUTURE THE COURT MAY NOT REVIEW PAPERS WHICH ARE EXCESSIVE IN LENGTH. ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR # 7489

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Continued To Jun-26-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Regulo Sierra, Tel. 415.672.3637, plaintiff/cross defendant and moving party, appearing on his own behalf;
Raymond Burke, Esq., Tel. 415.292.7230, for cross complainants and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT REGULO SIERRA's Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint is denied. To the extent that Cross-Defendant has filed an anti-SLAPP motion per CCP 425.16, Cross-Defendant presents no law or evidence to establish that the cross-complaint is directed at protected activity. (CCP 425.16(b). See Points & Auth. p. 5:19-24.) When one has agreed not to pursue future litigation, doing so is not protected activity for purposes of the anti-SLAPP statute. Answer must be filed by June 10, 2013. One Order signed for both motions. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Regulo Sierra, Tel. 415.672.3637, plaintiff/cross defendant and moving party, appearing on his own behalf;
Raymond Burke, Esq., Tel. 415.292.7230, for cross complainants and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT REGULO SIERRA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled. Cross-Complainants have pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for breach of contract. The First Amended Cross-Complaint does not appear time-barred on its face. A demurrer challenges defects that appear on the face of the pleading or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Cross-Defendant's arguments that the First Amended Cross-Complaint is a form of financial elder abuse (Points & Auth., p. 5:14-18), moot (id. at p. 6:2-5), time-barred (id. at p. 6:7-14), unconscionable (id. at p. 6:16 -7:2), and (4) unripe (id. at p. 7:4-6) do not appear on the face of the First Amended Cross-Complaint, but rather are based on extrinsic facts that are not judicially noticeable. Evidence that is not contained in the cross-complaint is improper on demurrer and not considered by the Court. Answer must be filed by June 10, 2013. One Order signed for both motions. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2012 Continued To Oct-03-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Mtn For Reconsideration Of Ruling Of Demurrer
Off Calendar Jul-26-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Raymond Burke, Esq. appearing specially for the defendants;
Patricia Hewlett and Michael Murauski non-parties to the case were present in court;
No other appearance.

The court informed Miss Hewlett and Mr. Murauski that it is illegal for someone who is not an attorney to represent a party in a lawsuit. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF REGULO SIERRA's Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling Of Demurrer is Denied. Plaintiff does not present any new law or facts upon which to base the reconsideration motion. The motion fails to comply with CCP 1008. Plaintiff's motion to add Judge Kahn as a doe defendant is also denied. It appears that Mr Sierra's complaint with Judge Kahn is that Judge Kahn decided earlier motions incorrectly; judicial immunity bars such claims in any event. Mr. Sierra also appears to make a motion to disqualify Judge Kahn under CCP 170.6. While that is moot for this motion, decided by a different judge, the motion is denied as untimely, brought long after Mr. Sierra learned Judge Kahn would hear matters in this case. Mr. Sierra is reminded that his papers are excessive in length. See California Rules of Court 3.1103 (d)(15 pages for opening memorandum and 20 pages for reply papers). I have in any event reviewed these papers, but in the future the Court may not review papers which are excessive in length. Orders signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489
